title: HOG configuration resource
description: One Kubernetes-style resource document from a HOG config file/stream. A HOG config is a `---`-separated multi-document YAML stream where each non-empty document matches this schema; `kind` selects which spec shape applies (see the `allOf` conditionals below). A full config must contain exactly one `Gateway` resource and, conventionally, one `Telemetry` resource plus any number of Route/RouteGroup/IdP/Policy/RequestPlugin/ResponsePlugin resources — that cross-document cardinality is enforced by HOG at load time, not by this per-document schema.

apiVersion.description: Conventionally "hog.dev/v1". HOG itself never reads or validates this field — it exists only so external tooling that cares about apiVersion/kind (kustomize, kubectl-style tooling) can identify and patch HOG resources.

kind.description: The resource type. Determines which shape `spec` must have.

spec.description: Resource-specific configuration; its shape depends on `kind` (see the allOf conditionals below).

stringMap.description: A map of string keys to string values.

metadata.description: The common identity block of every resource.

name.description: The resource's name, used to reference it from other resources (e.g. a Route's access.authorize lists Policy names) and as its display identifier.

labels.description: Arbitrary key/value labels. Matched by RouteGroup/plugin selectors, the same pattern as Kubernetes label selectors matching Pods.

operator.description: How to compare the label's value (if present) against `values`. Any other operator string never matches (fails closed).

values.description: Candidate values for In/NotIn. Unused (may be omitted) for Exists/DoesNotExist.

selector.description: A Kubernetes-style label selector. The zero value (no terms at all) matches everything.

matchLabels.description: Exact-match label set; every key/value pair must match (logical AND).

matchExpressions.description: Set-based selector terms, ANDed with matchLabels and with each other.

sessionProjectionGroups.description: Overrides the projected groups header name.

header.description: Request header name the rendered group list is injected as (default derives from identity.groups.as).

sessionProjection.description: Overrides the derive-from-passport session-claim projection defaults.

claims.description: Explicit claim name -> request header name mapping (replaces the default "one X-User-<Claim> header per passport claim" projection).

projectionConfig.description: Customizes identity-header projection onto the outbound request.

request.description: Reserved; currently has no effect. Accepted for forward-compatibility.

accessSpec.description: Access-control block: authentication requirement, authorization policy references, and identity projection. Applies to a Route or a RouteGroup.

auth.description: Whether a session/bearer principal is required. Defaults to "required" for service-type routes and "public" for app-type routes.

authorize.description: Names of `kind: Policy` resources to enforce. A route's own list is unioned (not replaced) with every matching RouteGroup's.

dir.description: Directory served (traversal-safe, built on os.Root). Required.

index.description: Filename served for the directory root and, on a miss, the SPA fallback shell. Defaults to "index.html".

spaFallback.description: On a miss for an extensionless path, serve `index` instead of 404, so client-side routing can own its own URLs. Defaults to true.

stripPrefix.description: Prefix trimmed from the inbound path before resolving it under `dir`.

cacheControl.description: Cache-Control header value set on served files.

upstream.description: Base URL of the backend (must parse as a URL with scheme and host). Required.

stripPrefix.description: Trimmed from the outbound path before proxying.

preserveHost.description: Forward the inbound Host header instead of the upstream's. Defaults to false.

forwardAccessToken.description: Inject Authorization: Bearer <access token> from the session principal onto the outbound request. Defaults to false.

forwardCookies.description: Pass the inbound Cookie header through to the upstream. Defaults to false.

timeout.description: Per-request timeout, a Go duration string (e.g. "10s"). A timeout returns 504; any other proxy error returns 502.

insecureSkipVerify.description: Disable upstream TLS certificate verification. Use only for trusted internal backends with self-signed certs. Defaults to false.

handlerApiBackend.description: A single backend call aggregated by a handler.type: api route.

group.description: JSON key this backend's response is merged under. Required; must be unique within the handler.

upstream.description: Base URL of this backend (must parse as a URL with scheme and host). Required.

path.description: Request path, joined onto the upstream. Supports {name} placeholders resolved from the route's own path parameters. Required.

method.description: HTTP method for the backend call. Defaults to GET.

required.description: If true (the default), a failed/timed-out call fails the whole aggregated request (502/504). If false, the backend is omitted from the merged body on failure and its group name is listed in the X-Hog-Partial response header instead.

forwardQuery.description: Forward the inbound request's query string to this backend. Defaults to false.

forwardAccessToken.description: Inject Authorization: Bearer <access token> for this backend call only. Defaults to false.

handlerApi.description: Built-in API-aggregation handler config (handler.type: api): fans out to multiple backends concurrently and merges their JSON responses under one key per backend.

timeout.description: Overall request timeout, a Go duration string (e.g. "5s"), applied to every backend call via a shared context.

backends.description: The backends to call and merge. At least one is required.

handlerHealth.description: Built-in health-check handler config (handler.type: health). Always returns a fixed {"status":"ok"} body; takes no configuration.

handler.description: Selects a terminal handler module and carries its config. Built-in types: static, reverse-proxy, api, health (validated below); any other value is a custom TerminalHandler module registered by a HOG plugin and is accepted as-is (its config is opaque to this schema).

type.description: Built-in: static, reverse-proxy, api, health; extensible via TerminalHandler plugin modules.

routeSpec.description: kind: Route spec — a single routable endpoint.

match.description: A net/http.ServeMux pattern. Trailing slash means a subtree match. Must be unique across all routes. Required.

type.description: The route's kind. If omitted, inferred from handler.type: reverse-proxy/api infer "service"; everything else infers "app".

routeGroupSpec.description: kind: RouteGroup spec — applies a shared type/access block to every Route whose labels match `selector`, the same pattern as a Kubernetes Service selecting Pods.

type.description: Default route type applied to matching routes that don't set their own `type`.

pluginSpec.description: kind: RequestPlugin / kind: ResponsePlugin spec — a chain-stage module instance selected by `type` (a registered plugin module name) and wired to matching routes via `selector`. No built-in request/response plugin types ship in HOG itself.

type.description: The registered plugin module name to instantiate. Required; no built-in types.

selector.description: Routes this plugin instance applies to. The zero value (no terms) matches every route.

config.description: Opaque configuration passed verbatim to the registered plugin module's factory.

issuer.description: The provider's issuer URL, used for OIDC discovery (<issuer>/.well-known/openid-configuration). Required.

clientID.description: The registered OAuth2 client ID. Required.

clientSecret.description: The registered OAuth2 client secret. Required.

redirectURL.description: Must match a redirect URI registered with the provider. Its path becomes HOG's callback route automatically. Required.

bearerAudience.description: Overrides the audience used to verify Bearer access tokens. Defaults to clientID if unset.

scopes.description: Requested OAuth2 scopes. Defaults to [openid, profile, email] if omitted.

pkce.description: Use PKCE (S256 code challenge) on the authorization code exchange. Defaults to true.

idpSpec.description: kind: IdP spec — an identity-provider connector. HOG supports exactly one active IdP resource today. The entire spec (including `type`) is handed to the registered IdP module's factory, the same polymorphic pattern as a Route's handler, so a custom (non-"oidc") type's fields are accepted as-is. "oidc" is the only built-in type and is validated below.

type.description: Built-in: oidc; extensible via custom IdP modules.

gatewaySessionConfig.description: The encrypted, HttpOnly session cookie (stateless BFF mode). Omit this whole block to run HOG as a pure reverse-proxy/aggregation gateway with no login flow.

key.description: Must be exactly 32 bytes once resolved — used as an AES-256-GCM key. Required if `session` is configured.

ttl.description: Session lifetime, a Go duration string. Defaults to "8h".

fingerprintHeaders.description: Request headers hashed into the session fingerprint. Defaults to ["User-Agent"].

infoPath.description: Path serving the current session's public info. Defaults to "/auth/session".

postLogoutRedirect.description: Path/URL to redirect to after logout. Defaults to "/".

gatewayIdentityGroups.description: How the userinfo/ID-token group list is filtered and rendered.

source.description: The userinfo/ID-token claim holding the group-DN (or group-name) array, e.g. isMemberOf.

match.description: Case-insensitive substrings; only entries containing at least one of these are kept. A groups block with no match entries never matches anything (fail-closed).

render.description: "cn" extracts the cn= component of each kept DN; "dn" keeps the whole DN/entry string unchanged. Defaults to "cn".

as.description: Session field name / default projected-header name for the rendered group list. Defaults to "groups".

gatewayIdentityConfig.description: The claim/group projection model shared by cookie sessions and Bearer auth. Omitting this block uses HOG's defaults (claims: [email, name, given_name, family_name], no groups, userInfo: auto).

claims.description: Passport claim allowlist persisted into the session/principal (sub is always kept separately). Defaults to [email, name, given_name, family_name].

userInfo.description: When to call the userinfo endpoint: "auto" fetches only when a configured claim/group source is missing from the token, "always"/"never" force the behavior. Defaults to "auto".

gatewayCSRFConfig.description: Configures net/http.CrossOriginProtection. CSRF protection is on by default.

enabled.description: Enables Fetch-metadata CSRF defense. Defaults to true; set false to disable entirely.

trustedOrigins.description: Origins to trust for a same-site-but-cross-origin browser SPA (e.g. a frontend on a different subdomain than this gateway).

bypassPatterns.description: Insecure CSRF-bypass path patterns, passed to CrossOriginProtection.AddInsecureBypassPattern. Use sparingly.

gatewayHSTSConfig.description: Configures the Strict-Transport-Security header. On by default.

enabled.description: Sets Strict-Transport-Security. Defaults to true; set false to disable.

maxAge.description: max-age in seconds. Defaults to 31536000 (1 year).

includeSubDomains.description: Adds the includeSubDomains directive. Defaults to true.

preload.description: Adds the preload directive. Defaults to false.

gatewayHeadersConfig.description: Configures the static security response headers. Set a string field to "" to omit that header entirely.

frameOptions.description: X-Frame-Options value. Defaults to "DENY"; set to "" to omit the header.

contentTypeOptions.description: X-Content-Type-Options value. Defaults to "nosniff"; set to "" to omit the header.

referrerPolicy.description: Referrer-Policy value. Defaults to "strict-origin-when-cross-origin"; set to "" to omit the header.

contentSecurityPolicy.description: Content-Security-Policy value. HOG sets no default CSP (it depends on the frontend served) — opt in explicitly.

gatewaySecurityConfig.description: CSRF protection and security response headers, applied gateway-wide as the outermost wrapper around every response (routes and the raw /auth/* endpoints alike).

gatewayAuthConfig.description: Paths for the built-in login/logout endpoints.

loginPath.description: Path that starts the OIDC login flow. Defaults to "/auth/login".

logoutPath.description: Path that ends the session and (if supported by the IdP) starts RP-initiated logout. Defaults to "/auth/logout".

gatewayStateProviderConfig.description: Configures the stateful session backend (e.g. for server-side refresh-token storage). Omit entirely to run in pure stateless-cookie mode.

type.description: The registered StateProvider plugin module name to instantiate. Required; no built-in types ship in HOG itself.

refreshSkew.description: How early before the access token's expiry to silently refresh, a Go duration string. Defaults to "60s"; must not be negative.

keyPrefix.description: Key prefix used by the state provider's storage. Defaults to "hog:sess:".

config.description: Opaque configuration passed verbatim to the registered StateProvider plugin factory.

gatewaySpec.description: kind: Gateway spec — the root resource. A config must contain exactly one.

listen.description: Address net/http listens on. Defaults to ":8080" if omitted.

trustedProxies.description: CIDRs/bare IPs trusted to set X-Forwarded-For/-Proto/-Host/-Port, X-Real-Ip, and Forwarded. A request from any other peer has those headers stripped before routing/tracing ever see it. Defaults to trusting no peer.

plugins.description: Build-time module manifest: Go import paths of plugin packages to link into the binary. Parsed here but consumed by the hog-build tool, not at gateway runtime.

endpoint.description: The OTLP collector endpoint (an http/https URL with a host). Omit to disable OTLP export.

headers.description: Custom headers sent with every OTLP export request (e.g. authentication).

insecure.description: Disable OTLP transport TLS. Defaults to false.

timeout.description: Export timeout, a Go duration string.

telemetryServiceConfig.description: Sets the OpenTelemetry resource service.name/service.version attributes.

name.description: The OpenTelemetry service.name resource attribute. Required.

telemetrySamplingConfig.description: The head, parent-based trace sampling ratio.

ratio.description: Fraction of traces sampled, 0.0-1.0. Defaults to 1.0 (sample everything).

level.description: Level the access-log line is emitted at. Defaults to "info".

properties.description: Which fields to include on each access-log line. An explicit list fully replaces the default set (no merge). Defaults to [method, path, status, duration_ms, client_ip, request_id, trace_id, span_id].

headers.description: Custom field name -> request header name to capture onto the access-log line. Header names authorization, cookie, proxy-authorization, and set-cookie (case-insensitive) are rejected to prevent credential leakage.

fields.description: Static custom fields added to every access-log line (field name -> value; ${VAR} is resolved at config load time).

telemetrySpec.description: kind: Telemetry spec — OpenTelemetry export + access-log settings. At most one Telemetry resource is allowed per config.

logLevel.description: The application's slog level. Defaults to "info".

policyRequire.description: The built-in (Tier A) group/claim rule. Must not be empty if present: at least one group or claim entry is required.

groups.description: Any-of: the principal must belong to at least one listed group.

claims.description: All-of across keys; each value is a scalar or list of acceptable values (any-of within that claim).

policyRego.description: Embedded OPA/Rego rule (Tier B), an alternative (or addition) to `require`.

path.description: File or directory of .rego source, resolved relative to where the config is loaded from. Must define `deny` under `package hog.authz`. Required.

policySpec.description: kind: Policy spec — referenced from access.authorize on a Route or RouteGroup. A policy is satisfied iff require passes (when present) AND the rego evaluation produces no deny (when present). At least one of require/rego is required.
